name: Waikiki Aquarium
tagline: The third-oldest aquarium in the US, right on the Waikiki shoreline — small enough to not overwhelm little ones.
parking: Limited street parking on Kalakaua Ave; paid lot at Kapiolani Park nearby
bestTime: Weekday mornings for fewest crowds; feeding times posted at entrance
kamaaina: Kamaaina rate with Hawaii ID — significantly cheaper than regular admission
talkStory: Founded in 1904, the Waikiki Aquarium is the third-oldest public aquarium in the United States, operated by the University of Hawaii. It was the first aquarium to breed the chambered nautilus, a deep-sea living fossil unchanged for 500 million years -- specimens are still on display. The outdoor monk seal exhibit houses rescued seals that cannot return to the wild. Its compact size is actually ideal for families with toddlers who get overwhelmed at larger venues.
